title: CropGraph Pest Species Catalog
description:
Slug-keyed scientific names for every pest and pathogen referenced from pest-disease.json. Each entry is the canonical name authority for one pest or disease slug, separated from the crop-association edges in pest-disease.json so that a single (slug, name) pair is curated once even when the same agent appears against many crops. Loader validates bidirectional referential integrity at module load: every distinct `pest` slug in pest-disease.json must have a row here, and every row here must be referenced by at least one entry in pest-disease.json.

Scope: covers pests (insects, mites, mollusks, nematodes, vertebrates) and diseases (fungi, oomycetes, bacteria, viruses) with their accepted scientific names. Physiological disorders (e.g. blossom-end-rot, sunscald, catfacing, tipburn, bitter-pit, prussic-acid-poisoning) are not biological agents; their `scientificName` is null with an explicit disorder note in the per-entry `source` field.

Evidence grounding: GBIF Backbone Taxonomy, UC IPM Online, Cornell Cooperative Extension, UF/IFAS Featured Creatures, EPPO Global Database, ICTV Master Species List, USDA-ARS, Penn State Extension. Names follow the modern accepted binomial where the literature has settled; for generic slugs that cover a multi-species complex (e.g. `cutworm`, `powdery-mildew`, `damping-off`) the name is given as `Genus spp.` or `Family spp.` with the dominant member species enumerated in the source citation.

version.description: Schema version. Bump on breaking changes only.

source.description: Provenance / curation block for the whole fixture.

license.description: License under which the data is redistributed (e.g. CC-BY-4.0).

lastUpdated.description: ISO date of the last hand-curation pass.

entries.description: One row per unique pest or disease slug. Order is not significant; helpers index by slug at load time.

slug.description: Kebab-case identifier matching the `pest` slug in pest-disease.json. Unique within the fixture.

commonName.description: Display name for the gardener. Title-case. For slugs that carry a structural prefix (e.g. `aphid-cabbage`), the common name is reordered to read naturally (`Cabbage Aphid`).

scientificName.description: Accepted scientific name. Binomial or higher-rank Latin name; pathovars and formae speciales use the ISPP infrasubspecific convention (`pv.`, `f.sp.`). Genus complexes that cover multiple species under one slug use `Genus spp.` with the dominant species enumerated in `source`. Null only for physiological disorders that are not biological agents; the disorder is then identified in `source`.

source.description: Taxonomic authority for the scientific name. One of GBIF Backbone Taxonomy, UC IPM Online, Cornell Cooperative Extension, UF/IFAS Featured Creatures, EPPO Global Database, ICTV Master Species List, USDA-ARS, Penn State Extension, or a combination separated by semicolons. For disorders, an explicit disorder note followed by the extension citation.
